etymology.description: Depicts a bird flying upwards -> modern form resembles a tasseled 升(升) (shēng, "ascend")
etymology.interpretation: The traditional form 飛(fēi, "fly") originally depicted a bird ascending, capturing the essence of flight. This reflects its meaning as visualized through the image of the flying bird.
etymology.simplification: The simplified form 飞(fēi, "fly") streamlines the depiction by reducing the strokes of the flying bird while still capturing the essence of flight to convey its meaning.

examples.chinese: 鸟在天空中飞翔(鳥在天空中飛翔)。
examples.english: The bird is flying in the sky.

examples.chinese: 他梦想着有一天能飞上蓝天(他夢想著有一天能飛上藍天)。
examples.pinyin: Tā mèngxiǎngzhe yǒu yītiān néng fēi shàng lántiān.
examples.english: He dreams of flying up to the blue sky one day.

examples.chinese: 飞机在云层间快速飞行(飛機在雲層間快速飛行)。
examples.pinyin: Fēijī zài yúncéng jiān kuàisù fēixíng.
examples.english: The plane flies quickly between the clouds.
